title: EntryAnalyzer service + background analysis on tracker entry creation
context: See docs/work-dump-analysis-design.md. Two parts: (1) Create app/services/entry_analyzer.py — LLM-powered analysis of tracker entries that creates inbox suggestions. Uses httpx to call OpenAI-compatible API, returns 0-3 suggestions, creates InboxItems. Gracefully skips if no API key. (2) Add `analyzed` bool to TrackerEntry model/schema. Wire background analysis into POST /tracker/entries via asyncio.create_task. Background task gets its own DB session.
acceptance: 1) app/services/entry_analyzer.py exists with EntryAnalyzer class. 2) LLM prompt analyzes entry text and returns structured suggestions. 3) Suggestions become InboxItems with clear source attribution. 4) analyzed field on TrackerEntry, set True after processing. 5) POST /tracker/entries fires background analysis (doesn't block response). 6) No API key configured → silent skip, no crash. 7) Background task uses its own DB session (not the request session). 8) Tests with mocked LLM for: suggestions created, empty response, no API key, analyzed flag.
